item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to help me think through a new audit trail feature for this repo. I know the goal but not the right UX or scope yet.
success_definition: Routes to brainstorming rather than planning or work.
Explains that behavior and scope need to be clarified first.
Preserves the path onward into planning.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to figure out the strongest next bets for this plugin before we pick one to build.
item.expected_handoff: shortlist, then $flywheel:brainstorm for the selected idea
success_definition: Routes to ideate rather than brainstorm or plan.
Frames ideation as pre-work before the main loop.
Keeps the selected idea flowing into brainstorming.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start on docs/brainstorms/2026-04-18-audit-trail-requirements.md and get me ready to build it.
success_definition: Routes to planning from an existing requirements doc.
Does not send the user back into brainstorming without a concrete reason.
Preserves the path into work once the plan is ready.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start on docs/plans/2026-04-18-001-feat-audit-trail-plan.md and start implementing it.
success_definition: Routes directly to work from an existing plan.
Acknowledges review and shipping as part of the downstream path.
Includes spin as an offered end-of-session capture step.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to help before merge. The code is already changed and I want to find bugs, regressions, or missing tests.
item.expected_handoff: review findings, then push/PR or exit
success_definition: Routes to review rather than work or brainstorm.
Frames review as pre-merge risk finding.
Keeps the downstream path compatible with merge or PR update.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to wrap up this completed task and preserve what we learned so the next task is easier.
success_definition: Routes to spin for a completed session.
Frames spin as capture of solved problems or durable guidance.
Does not reroute back into implementation.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start for a runtime-risky queue and retry change that is coded, reviewed, and now needs safe release planning before final shipping.
success_definition: Routes to rollout instead of flattening the change directly into shipping.
Frames rollout as release-planning and validation work rather than deployment automation.
Keeps shipping as the next handoff once rollout posture is defined.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start for a live production issue that currently has logs, metrics, and customer-impact reports but no confirmed fix path yet.
item.expected_handoff: incident brief, then debug/rollout/ship as needed
success_definition: Routes to incident handling instead of ordinary debug or work.
Frames the job as runtime-evidence intake and decision-making.
Keeps the next handoff explicit once mitigation vs rollback vs patch is chosen.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:start to rename the stale config key in this repo. It is a small, well-defined change and we already know the target behavior.
success_definition: Allows a direct-to-work path for a clear small change.
Does not force brainstorming just for ceremony.
Still preserves review and spin later in the path.
